title: The Sound of Molecules
scientific_question: Can a structured mapping from molecular properties to musical representations recover stable and interpretable chemical organization rather than arbitrary aesthetic analogy?
paper_role: creative cross-domain case with quantitative validation

full_system_arm.label: ScienceClaw full workflow
full_system_arm.description: Multi-agent cross-domain workflow spanning chemistry, sonification, music analysis, and pattern discovery.
full_system_arm.result_summary: Final CS1 report embedding achieved retrieval@3=0.271 (95% bootstrap CI 0.083-0.500) and same-class nearest-neighbor rate=0.688 (95% CI 0.062-0.812) on the fixed 16-compound manifest.

benchmark_arms.label: Strong single-agent baseline
benchmark_arms.description: One agent with the same model family, same compounds, same descriptor access, and matched tool budget.
benchmark_arms.result_summary: Strong physchem baseline (2D + 3D cosine) achieved retrieval@3=0.271, same-class nearest-neighbor rate=0.188, ARI=0.179.

benchmark_arms.label: Scripted cheminformatics baseline
benchmark_arms.description: Fixed descriptor extraction and similarity workflow without adaptive multi-agent continuation.
benchmark_arms.result_summary: Morgan-fingerprint Tanimoto baseline achieved retrieval@3=0.292 and same-class nearest-neighbor rate=0.312.

benchmark_arms.label: ScienceClaw ablation: no multi-agent specialization
benchmark_arms.description: Collapse chemistry, music, and comparison roles into a single generalized analysis flow while keeping the same core data sources.
benchmark_arms.result_summary: No-3D sonic ablation achieved retrieval@3=0.250, same-class nearest-neighbor rate=0.375, and nearest-neighbor agreement 0.625 versus the full embedding.

controls.label: Shuffled compound-label control
controls.purpose: Check whether apparent class structure survives label randomization.
controls.result_summary: Observed retrieval@3=0.271 versus shuffled-label null mean=0.125 (95th percentile=0.229, permutation p=0.0178).

controls.label: Shuffled composer-label control
controls.purpose: Check whether composer-era assignments are stronger than random relabeling.
controls.result_summary: Observed class-majority era purity=0.833 versus shuffled-composer-label null mean=0.810.

controls.label: Random descriptor-to-harmony mapping control
controls.purpose: Test whether the proposed mapping outperforms arbitrary sonification transforms.
controls.result_summary: Observed retrieval@3=0.271 versus random sonification null mean=0.224 (95th percentile=0.312).

metrics.label: Drug-class retrieval@k
metrics.notes: Quantify whether harmonic embeddings preserve pharmacological class neighborhoods. k=3; bootstrap 95% CI=0.083-0.500; permutation p=0.0178.

metrics.label: Same-class nearest-neighbor rate
metrics.notes: Fraction of compounds whose nearest neighbor in the harmonic embedding shares the same pharmacological class. Bootstrap 95% CI=0.062-0.812; permutation p=0.0004.

metrics.label: Mapping robustness across descriptor sets
metrics.notes: Stability of qualitative structure across 2D and optional 3D/conformer descriptor choices. Nearest-neighbor agreement vs no-3D ablation=0.625; report-alignment=0.125.

metrics.label: Rerun stability
metrics.notes: How stable the outputs are across repeated runs. Deterministic conformer seeds and fixed benchmark manifest.

metrics.label: Human intervention time
metrics.notes: Hands-on time required to obtain paper-quality outputs. No manual intervention required once the compound manifest was fixed.

metrics.label: Wall-clock runtime
metrics.notes: End-to-end time for the workflow. Measured by the benchmark runner.

cross_case_summary.baseline_gap: Retrieval@3 is -0.021 versus the strongest chemistry baseline, while same-class nearest-neighbor rate is +0.375 above the best baseline.
cross_case_summary.robustness: Full-system retrieval@3 beat shuffled-label and random-mapping nulls; ARI=0.083, NMI=0.557, scaffold-held-out accuracy=0.500 on 16 evaluated compounds.
cross_case_summary.human_involvement: none once the 16-compound manifest and deterministic feature pipeline were fixed
failure_modes: Potential sensitivity to handcrafted descriptor-to-harmony choices
cross_case_summary.where_scienceclaw_helps: The richer sonic embedding improves same-class neighborhood coherence and exposes hidden same-class pairs that are weak under 2D fingerprints.
cross_case_summary.where_simple_workflows_suffice: Purely chemical retrieval remains competitive or better for obvious analog-retrieval tasks, especially on a small fixed panel.
cross_case_summary.paper_takeaway: CS1 is strongest as a structure-recovery case: the sonification-derived embedding retains non-random class structure under nulls and uncertainty analysis, but it should not be framed as uniformly outperforming standard cheminformatics.
